Player.title: Player
Player.description: Represents a player in the Fut7Stats application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Player entity.

Match.title: Match
Match.description: Represents a match in the Fut7Stats application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Match entity.

teamAScore.description: The score of team A in the match.

teamBScore.description: The score of team B in the match.

teamAPlayerIds.description: References to Players in team A. (Relationship: Match 1:N Player)

teamBPlayerIds.description: References to Players in team B. (Relationship: Match 1:N Player)

MatchEvent.title: MatchEvent
MatchEvent.description: Represents an event that occurred during a match (e.g., goal, assist, foul).

id.description: Unique identifier for the MatchEvent entity.

eventType.description: The type of event that occurred (e.g., goal, assist, foul).

eventTime.description: The time in the match when the event occurred (e.g., in minutes).

PlayerMatchRating.title: PlayerMatchRating
PlayerMatchRating.description: Represents a player's rating for a specific match.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PlayerMatchRating entity.

rating.description: The player's rating for the match (1-10).

raterPlayerId.description: Reference to Player who rated this match. (Relationship: Player 1:N PlayerMatchRating)

definition.description: Collection to store admin roles. Existence of a document with the user's UID grants admin privileges.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the player.

params.name: matchId
params.description: The unique identifier of the match.

definition.description: Subcollection to store events within a match.

params.name: matchId
params.description: The unique identifier of the match.

params.name: matchEventId
params.description: The unique identifier of the match event.

definition.description: Collection to store the rate that a player has in a match.

params.name: playerMatchRatingId
params.description: The unique identifier of the player match rating.

firestore.reasoning:
Given the requirement for a single administrator to manage players and matches, I propose a structure that leverages a dedicated collection for admin role and path-based ownership to manage player and match data.

**Authorization Independence:**

*   The structure utilizes a dedicated `/roles_admin/{uid}` collection to store admin roles. This avoids the need for `get()` calls to parent documents to determine admin status.

**Structural Segregation:**

*   Players and matches are stored in dedicated collections.

**Access Modeling:**

*   **Global Roles (DBAC):** The `/roles_admin/{uid}` collection is used to grant admin privileges based on document existence.
*   Data relating to matches is stored in the `/matches/{matchId}` and `/matches/{matchId}/match_events/{matchEventId}` paths. Player rating data is stored in `/player_match_ratings/{playerMatchRatingId}`.

**QAPs Support:**

*   `list` operations on `/players` and `/matches` can be secured by checking for admin privileges in the rules. This structure supports QAPs because authorization is based on role existence, not data content. The `list` operation will only return the data if the requesting user has admin privileges.
